# UC-19.1.48 · Dell VxRail vSAN Unhealthy vSAN Objects and Resync Backlog

> **Criticality:** Critical &middot; **Difficulty:** Advanced &middot; **Pillar:** Observability &middot; **Type:** Availability, Performance &middot; **Wave:** Walk &middot; **Status:** Verified

*Think of a RAID set after a disk was replaced: the new part is in, but a lot of data still has to be copied back to full safety. If a second part fails in the middle, the whole set can be lost. We watch the rebuild work left and whether anything is marked unreachable so people know when that risky time is open and for how long.*

---

## Description

Dell VxRail’s storage service is **VMware vSAN**: VMs are backed by **vSAN objects** and **components** on multiple hosts. This UC is the **object and resync** layer (Skyline / API `vsan_health`, `inaccessible_objects`, `inaccessible_vm_count` when available, `resync_bytes_remaining` with byte/MB/GB normalization). It is **not** a canonical Splunkbase sourcetype — you **define** `vmware:vsan:health` or `vmware:vsan:powercli` from **(A)** vCenter **REST** (`/api/session` then vSAN health per cluster) **(B)** **PowerCLI** **Get-VsanClusterHealth** + **Get-VsanResyncComponent** to HEC **(C)** vROps via **4748** field mapping **(D)** `vsan-health` logs from **ESXi**. The SPL **coalesce()**s REST, **PowerCLI**, and **vROps** field names, applies a **per-cluster** `vsan_resync_thresholds` lookup, tracks **inaccessible** time span in-window, classifies a **4-tier** `severity_bucket`, **fleet** **eventstats** for fleet dashboards, and **left-joins** **UC-19.1.47** `vxrail:disks` to label **RMA-driven** rebuilds versus **silent** object-layer issues.

## Value

A large **resync** means a **rebuild** window where a **second** fault can take VMs offline. Executives and service owners need to know **how long the risk window is** and when **I/O SLOs** are threatened — not just that a cluster "looks yellow" in a portal. Correlating **object** / **resync** telemetry with **UC-19.1.47** **drive** work prevents paging the wrong bridge (expected **RMA** **rebuild** vs new **object** / **network** / **witness** problem). UC-19.1.49 **SRS** is **Dell** **TSE** access, not a substitute for this **vCenter**-layer view.

## Implementation

Stand up **index=vxrail**; implement **(A)** Python REST→**HEC** or **(B)** **PowerCLI**→**HEC** at 300s; add `/etc/splunk-vcenter-creds.cnf` 0600; create `lookups/vsan_resync_thresholds.csv`; add props for `vmware:vsan:health` JSON. Save the **spl**; schedule `*/5 * * * *` on -2h; alert when `severity_bucket` in (critical,warning,watch) with 30m suppress on `cluster`+ tier. Add dashboard fleet tiles; drill to UC-19.1.47 on the same `cluster`.

## Detailed Implementation

### Prerequisites
- `index=vxrail`, HEC, Heavy Forwarder or Windows task host, vCenter 7.0U3+ with VsanHealth.View.
- `lookups/vsan_resync_thresholds.csv` (see Step 4).
- Sibling UCs: UC-19.1.47 drives; UC-19.1.49 SRS; complementary scope.

**Step 1 — Configure data collection**

A — vCenter REST → HEC (recommended)

(1) Service account: grant Read-only and VsanHealth.View; include Cluster inventory to list vSAN clusters.

(2) Credential file mode 0600: `/etc/splunk-vcenter-creds.cnf` with `VCENTER_FQDN`, `VCENTER_USER`, `VCENTER_PASS`, `HEC_URL`, `HEC_TOKEN`, optional `VERIFY_CA` to your enterprise CA bundle.

(3) Python poller (60–100 lines) — `requests`, `POST /api/session`, `vmware-api-session-id`, `GET /api/vcenter/cluster`, then for each `cluster` `GET /api/vcenter/vsan/clusters/{cluster}/health` (vSphere 8+ path; 7.x may differ — use vCenter Developer Center / API Explorer; 6.7-era paths live under older `/rest/.../vsan` namespaces). Flatten to one JSON per cluster, HEC `event` with sourcetype `vmware:vsan:health`.

```python
#!/opt/splunk/bin/python3
"""vcenter_vsan_to_hec.py — vSAN health to HEC; align JSON keys to your vCenter API Explorer output."""
import json, time, requests

def read_cnf(path):
    o = {}
    with open(path) as f:
        for line in f:
            s = line.strip()
            if not s or s.startswith("#") or "=" not in s:
                continue
            k, v = s.split("=", 1)
            o[k.strip()] = v.strip()
    return o

def hec(cnf, ev, st="vmware:vsan:health"):
    body = json.dumps({
        "sourcetype": st,
        "source": "vcenter:vsan:health",
        "time": int(time.time()),
        "event": ev,
    })
    r = requests.post(
        cnf["HEC_URL"],
        data=body,
        headers={"Authorization": "Splunk " + cnf["HEC_TOKEN"], "Content-Type": "application/json"},
        timeout=300,
        verify=cnf.get("VERIFY_CA") or True,
    )
    r.raise_for_status()

def main():
    c = read_cnf("/etc/splunk-vcenter-creds.cnf")
    s = c["VCENTER_FQDN"]
    se = requests.Session()
    se.auth = (c["VCENTER_USER"], c["VCENTER_PASS"])
    v = c.get("VERIFY_CA") or True
    s_url = "https://%s/api/session" % s
    h = se.post(s_url, verify=v, timeout=120)
    h.raise_for_status()
    tok = h.json().get("value") or h.headers.get("vmware-api-session-id")
    hdrs = {"vmware-api-session-id": str(tok), "Accept": "application/json"}
    base = "https://%s" % s
    for row in se.get("%s/api/vcenter/cluster" % base, headers=hdrs, verify=v, timeout=120).json():
        cid = row.get("cluster")
        if not cid:
            continue
        rh = se.get(
            "%s/api/vcenter/vsan/clusters/%s/health" % (base, cid),
            headers=hdrs,
            verify=v,
            timeout=180,
        )
        if rh.status_code == 404:
            continue
        if rh.status_code != 200:
            hec(c, {"cluster_id": cid, "err_http": rh.status_code, "err_body": rh.text[:400]})
            continue
        H = rh.json()
        overall = H.get("health") or H.get("clusterHealth") or H.get("overall") or {}
        hl = overall.get("overallHealth") or overall.get("OverallHealth") or overall.get("health") or {}
        stv = (hl.get("health") or hl.get("value") or hl.get("status") or "unknown")
        hec(
            c,
            {
                "cluster_id": cid,
                "vsan_health": stv,
                "**inaccessible_objects**": (H.get("inaccessible_objects") or H.get("numInaccessibleObjects") or 0),
                "inaccessible_vm_count": (H.get("inaccessible_vm_count") or H.get("inaccessibleVms") or 0),
                "**resync_bytes_remaining**": (H.get("resync_bytes_remaining") or H.get("bytesRemainingToSync") or 0),
                "resync_unit": "bytes",
                "vsan_architecture": (H.get("vsanArch") or H.get("architecture") or "osa"),
            },
        )

if __name__ == "__main__":
    main()
```

(4) inputs.conf (Heavy Forwarder) — 300s interval: `[script://$SPLUNK_HOME/etc/apps/TA-VCenter-vSAN-HEC/bin/vcenter_vsan_to_hec.py]`, `interval=300`, `sourcetype=vmware:vsan:health`, `index=vxrail`.

(5) props.conf — `[vmware:vsan:health]`: `SHOULD_LINEMERGE=false`, `LINE_BREAKER=([\r\n]+)`, `TRUNCATE=20000`, `KV_MODE=none`, `INDEXED_EXTRACTIONS=json`. Add `STRPTIME` if you embed event time in JSON; otherwise HEC time is enough for ordering in early pilots.

B — PowerCLI → HEC

(1) Windows Task Scheduler or cron (PowerShell 7) with VMware PowerCLI 12.7+. `Connect-VIServer` with a read-only role that has VsanHealth.View; `Get-VsanClusterHealth` per vSAN cluster; `Get-VsanResyncComponent` piped to `Measure-Object -Property BytesToSync -Sum`. Emit one JSON per cluster, POST to HEC, `sourcetype=vmware:vsan:powercli`, same index.

(2) PowerShell sample (40–50 lines) — run under Task Scheduler every 5m as a gMSA; install `VMware.VimAutomation.Core` and `VMware.VimAutomation.vSAN` (PowerCLI 12.7+). Fill `$Vc` and HEC, then test with `-WhatIf` removed.

```powershell
# vsan_powercli_to_hec.ps1 — requires PowerShell 5.1+ or 7+
$ErrorActionPreference = "Stop"
Import-Module VMware.VimAutomation.Core, VMware.VimAutomation.vSan -ErrorAction Stop
# HEC: use JSON event wrapper identical to the Python poller
$HecUrl = "https://idx:8088/services/collector/event"
$HecTok = (Get-Content C:\\opt\\vcenter-cred\\hectoken.txt -Raw).Trim()
$vc = "vcenter.corp.local"
$secp = Get-Credential -Message "vCenter read-only"
Connect-VIServer -Server $vc -Credential $secp
foreach ($cl in Get-Cluster) {
  if (-not $cl.VsanEnabled) { continue }
  $h = Get-VsanClusterHealth -Cluster $cl.Name
  $res = Get-VsanResyncComponent -Cluster $cl.Name
  $sum = 0; if ($res) { $sum = ($res | Measure-Object -Property BytesToSync -Sum).Sum }
  if ($null -eq $sum) { $sum = 0 }
  $overall = "unknown"
  if ($h.OverallHealth) { $overall = $h.OverallHealth.ToString().ToLower() }
  $ev = @{
    cluster_id = $cl.Name
    vsan_health = $overall
    inaccessible_objects = 0
    resync_bytes_remaining = [long]$sum
    resync_unit = "bytes"
    vsan_architecture = "osa"
  } | ConvertTo-Json -Compress
  $body = '{"sourcetype":"vmware:vsan:powercli","source":"vcenter:vsan:powercli","time":' + [int](Get-Date -UFormat %s) + ',"event":' + $ev + "}"
  Invoke-RestMethod -Uri $HecUrl -Method Post -Headers @{ Authorization = "Splunk " + $HecTok; "Content-Type" = "application/json" } -Body $body
}
Disconnect-VIServer -Server $vc -Confirm:$false
```

C — Aria / vROps (Splunkbase 4748)

Install Splunk Add-on for VMware vRealize Operations Manager, map exported properties such as `vsan_overall_health` into the `coalesce()` list in the SPL, optionally `sourcetype=vmware:vsan:vrops`. Slower cadence; keep A or B as primary.

D — ESXi `vsan-health` logs (Splunkbase 4844/6692)

`[monitor:///var/log/vmware/vsan-health/*.log]` where support policy allows, or syslog relay to SC4S; `sourcetype=vmware:vsan:health:log`. Catches line-level Skyline and re-test context between five-minute pollers.

```ini
# Universal Forwarder on a supported ESXi file tail path only where VMware allows local UF
[monitor:///var/log/vmware/vsan-health/*.log]
disabled = 0
sourcetype = vmware:vsan:health:log
index = vxrail
# host = or crcSalt as your forwarder app standard; many sites use syslog to SC4S instead
```

Priority: A or B = authoritative numeric snapshot; C = optional enterprise NOC; D = drill-down signal.

**Step 2 — Create the search and alert**

Schedule cron `*/5 * * * *` on -2h; suppress 30m on cluster + severity_bucket transition.

(1) coalesce maps REST, PowerCLI, and vROps field spellings; src_kind tags the collector for duplicate detection (do not run A+B both to production without dedup design).

(2) vsan_resync_thresholds.csv provides critical (default 1 TiB), warning (100 GiB), watch (10 GiB) in bytes; per-cluster override without SPL forks.

(3) inacc_age_min = span inside the search window of events where `inacc>0` — a proxy for stuck inaccess; pair with a summary or KV first-seen for stricter SLOs if required.

(4) eventstats adds fleet cluster counts to each row for dashboard tiles.

(5) Left join to `vxrail:disks` (UC-19.1.47) shows PFA correlation and labels expected rebuild during RMA.

```spl
index=vxrail (sourcetype="vmware:vsan:health" OR sourcetype="vmware:vsan:powercli" OR sourcetype="vmware:vsan:health:log" OR sourcetype="vmware:vsan:vrops") earliest=-2h
| eval src_kind=case(sourcetype="vmware:vsan:health","rest", sourcetype="vmware:vsan:powercli","pcli", sourcetype="vmware:vsan:health:log","esxi_log", sourcetype="vmware:vsan:vrops","vrops", 1=1,"other")
| eval cluster=lower(coalesce(cluster_id, ClusterId, vsan_cluster_id, cluster_mo, cluster_moref, ClusterName, mo_id, "unknown"))
| eval health_raw=lower(trim(coalesce(vsan_health, OverallHealth, healthLevel, health_level, vsan_overall_health, vrops_vsan_status, "")))
| eval health_norm=case(match(health_raw,"^(healthy|green|ok)$"),"ok", match(health_raw,"^(info|blue|notice)$"),"info", match(health_raw,"^(warning|yellow|degraded|warn)$"),"warning", match(health_raw,"^(unhealthy|critical|red|error|failed|severe)$"),"critical", 1=1, health_raw)
| eval inacc=tonumber(coalesce(inaccessible_objects, InaccessibleObjects, ObjectsInaccessible, InaccessibleObjectCount, "0"))
| eval inacc_vm=tonumber(coalesce(inaccessible_vm_count, InaccessibleVmCount, inaccessibleVms, "0"))
| eval resync_bytes_raw=tonumber(coalesce(resync_bytes_remaining, ResyncBytesRemaining, BytesToSync, ResyncBytes, bytes_to_sync, "0"))
| eval resync_units=lower(coalesce(resync_unit, ResyncUnit, resync_uom, "bytes"))
| eval resync_bytes=if(resync_units="mb", resync_bytes_raw*1048576, if(resync_units="gb", resync_bytes_raw*1073741824, resync_bytes_raw))
| eval arch=lower(coalesce(vsan_architecture, VsanArchitecture, "osa"))
| eval is_stretched=tonumber(coalesce(is_stretched, IsStretched, witness_present, "0"))
| eval rebuild=tonumber(coalesce(rebuild_in_progress, RebuildInProgress, is_resync, "0"))
| where cluster!="unknown"
| stats values(src_kind) as sources, latest(health_norm) as health, max(inacc) as inacc, max(inacc_vm) as inacc_vm, max(resync_bytes) as resync_bytes, latest(arch) as arch, latest(is_stretched) as is_stretched, max(rebuild) as rebuild, latest(_time) as _time, earliest(eval(if(inacc>0, _time, null()))) as inacc_first, latest(eval(if(inacc>0, _time, null()))) as inacc_last by cluster
| eval inacc_age_min=if(inacc>0, round((inacc_last-inacc_first)/60,1), 0)
| lookup vsan_resync_thresholds.csv cluster OUTPUT critical_bytes, warning_bytes, watch_bytes, is_remote_consumer, stretched_inacc_bump
| eval is_remote=tonumber(coalesce(is_remote_consumer, "0"))
| eval inacc=if(is_remote=1 AND inacc>0, 0, inacc)
| eval stretched_bump=coalesce(tonumber(stretched_inacc_bump,10),0)
| eval critical_bytes=coalesce(tonumber(critical_bytes,10), 1099511627776)
| eval warning_bytes=coalesce(tonumber(warning_bytes,10), 107374182400)
| eval watch_bytes=coalesce(tonumber(watch_bytes,10), 10737418240)
| eval inacc_for_sev=if(is_stretched=1, inacc+stretched_bump, inacc)
| eval sev_inacc_crit=if(inacc_for_sev>0 AND inacc_age_min>=5,1,0)
| eval sev_inacc_warn=if(inacc_for_sev>0 AND inacc_age_min<5,1,0)
| eval severity_bucket=case(health="critical" OR sev_inacc_crit=1 OR resync_bytes>=critical_bytes OR inacc_vm>0, "critical", health="warning" OR sev_inacc_warn=1 OR (resync_bytes>=warning_bytes AND resync_bytes<critical_bytes), "warning", health="info" OR (resync_bytes>=watch_bytes AND resync_bytes<warning_bytes) OR rebuild=1, "watch", 1=1, "ok")
| where severity_bucket!="ok"
| eventstats count as fleet_cluster_rows, sum(eval(if(severity_bucket="critical",1,0))) as fleet_sev_critical, sum(eval(if(severity_bucket="warning",1,0))) as fleet_sev_warning, sum(eval(if(severity_bucket="watch",1,0))) as fleet_sev_watch
| eval resync_gb=round(resync_bytes/1073741824, 2)
| join type=left max=0 cluster [ search index=vxrail sourcetype="vxrail:disks" earliest=-4h | eval c=lower(coalesce(cluster_id,ClusterId,cluster_name,"unknown")) | eval pred=if(tonumber(coalesce(PredictiveFailureCount, predictive_failure, "0"))>0 OR match(lower(tostring(coalesce(predictive_failure,""))),"(?i)true|1|yes|pfa|predict"),1,0) | stats max(pred) as drive_predictive_active by c | rename c as cluster ]
| eval drive_vs_object_note=case(drive_predictive_active=1 AND resync_gb>0, "RMA/rebuild in progress (see UC-19.1.47)", drive_predictive_active=1, "Disk signals present; confirm object layer", resync_gb>0, "Resync without recent PFA; investigate", 1=1, "-")
| sort 0 - fleet_sev_critical - resync_gb
| table _time, fleet_cluster_rows, fleet_sev_critical, fleet_sev_warning, fleet_sev_watch, cluster, severity_bucket, health, inacc, inacc_age_min, inacc_vm, resync_gb, arch, is_stretched, drive_predictive_active, drive_vs_object_note, sources
```

**Step 3 — Validate**

(1) Sanity (steady state): `index=vxrail (sourcetype="vmware:vsan:health" OR sourcetype="vmware:vsan:powercli") earliest=-1h | stats latest(health) as h, max(inaccessible_objects) as inacc by cluster` — in a healthy build you want `h` in the `ok` family and `inacc=0`. If you always see `unknown` or `null`, the poller is not mapping the API JSON; capture one `/health` response in a lab and adjust field paths before paging production.

(2) Resync trend: `... same index/sourcetype | timechart span=15m max(resync_bytes_remaining) as maxb by cluster` — the series should rest near 0; it ramps during drive RMA, LCM, or **SPBM** work and trails off when complete. A flat high plateau for many hours is a Step 4 tune or Step 5 incident.

(3) Object inaccess age (proxy in standard SPL): after you run the first pipeline through `inacc` or reuse `inaccessible_objects` from raw events, `| stats earliest(eval(if(inacc>0,_time,null()))) as t0, latest(eval(if(inacc>0,_time,null()))) as t1, max(inacc) as m by cluster | where m>0 | eval inacc_age_min=round((t1-t0)/60,1)` — values that climb across consecutive validation runs without operator action are worse than a single five-minute blip. Pair with a manual vSAN Objects or Data view in vCenter to confirm the same object families.

(4) Cross-source agreement (A vs B only): `index=vxrail (sourcetype="vmware:vsan:health" OR sourcetype="vmware:vsan:powercli") earliest=-1h | stats values(health) as healths by cluster | where mvcount(healths)>1` — both collectors should not contradict for an entire hour unless one session is stale (failed poll that still posts), one points at a linked vCenter the operator forgot, or one normalizes enum casing differently. Fix credentials and session age before you tune thresholds.

(5) Sibling drive correlation (UC-19.1.47): run the UC-19.1.47 disk SPL and this UC’s vSAN SPL in the same time window, joined on a normalized `cluster` key, or use the left join already embedded in the main saved search. You expect `drive_predictive_active=1` and non-zero `resync_gb` during a documented RMA; you investigate if `resync_gb` is high with no disk signal and no change — that pattern points to object, network, or stale Skyline more than a missing FRU.

(6) ESA vs OSA tag: `... | stats latest(arch) as a by cluster` — when a is uniformly `esa`, tighten or widen the CSV in Step 4 after you observe a month of rebuilds; ESA and OSA can differ in how fast resync clears for the same nominal capacity class.

**Step 4 — Tune for your environment**

(1) Resync by cluster size and rebuild parallelism: a three-node vSAN has fewer parallel rebuild streams than an eight-node cluster, but it also has less aggregate network headroom. Document mean time to resync (MTR) from your last two controlled RMAs; set `critical_bytes` just above the P95 “good” resync for planned work and far below a runaway you never want to see without human eyes.

(2) vsan_resync_thresholds.csv — one row per `cluster` with `critical_bytes`, `warning_bytes`, `watch_bytes` (integers, bytes) and optional `stretched_inacc_bump`, `is_remote_consumer` (0/1). The SPL uses `tonumber` so leave cells empty to inherit defaults. Store the CSV in `lookups/` on the search head; push via deployer/CM in clustered search.

(3) LCM and change windows — re-use the same `vxrail_lcm_windows.csv` (or your enterprise change lookup) you standardize in UC-19.1.47. Either suppress watch-only in-window or degrade one severity tier with a documented governance rule; do not blanket-mute the index.

(4) Stretched/witness — the CSV bump simulates a higher acceptable `inacc` for partition noise; tune with your network and VMware SME, not a guess from day one. Keep the alert on; only thresholds move.

(5) Per-VM blast radius — when your vCenter poller can emit `inaccessible_vm_count`, the case path already pages critical on any positive count; if your feed lacks it, keep relying on `inacc` and document the gap in your runbook’s “known limitations”.

(6) vSAN **HCI Mesh** — for consumer clusters, when producer RFO is the real story, set `is_remote_consumer=1` in the CSV to avoid treating remote-side maintenance as a local RMA, and file the change on the producer schedule instead.

(7) ESA — vSphere 8+ ESA adds checks your 7.x OSA case list never saw; branch `severity_bucket` in `local` macros once a JSON head sample names the new fields. Keep OSA/ESA in one search via `arch`.

(8) Dashboard layout — keep fleet tiles (`fleet_sev_*`) in the top row, per-cluster table second, and a third row that embeds a subsearch or link to the UC-19.1.47 saved report filtered on `cluster` for the same `earliest` window so the NOC has one click from object story to drive story.

**Step 5 — Troubleshooting**

(1) HTTP 404 for `/api/vcenter/vsan/clusters/{cluster}/health` on an otherwise current VCSA — your build may not expose that exact resource, or the cluster MoRef is not vSAN-enabled. Confirm with API Explorer, verify vCenter version, and for 6.7-era estates follow VMware guidance for the older REST vSAN health paths. You would rather fail closed with a scripted error event than silently skip clusters.

(2) PowerCLI returns nothing from `Get-VsanClusterHealth` while the UI is fine — the service account role lacks `VsanHealth.View` or the login reached a vCenter with no vSAN Cluster in scope. `Get-VIPrivilege -Id VsanHealth.View` and a role PDF export to the change board fixes most cases.

(3) `vsan_health='warning'` for hours in calm steady state — **vSAN Skyline Health** can lag real remediation until a periodic or manual retest. Use the vSphere Retest in Skyline health views; on 8+ use the documented re-check operation if your org allows it; correlate `vmware:vsan:health:log` if you have D enabled.

(4) Resync bytes climbing in a planned drive RMA — expected; confirm UC-19.1.47 PFA, Dell work order, and LCM window. Suppress with the same MOP you used for the disk swap, not a global mute of `index=vxrail`.

(5) Resync stuck (no progress for many hours) with no obvious UI cause — cross-check UC-19.1.47 for new PFA or offline on the destination or source hosts; an iDRAC SEL read often beats a pure object-layer first guess.

(6) `inaccessible_objects=0` but UI shows critical — Skyline may be flagging non-object health (for example network or vmk partition with ping test failures) while object counts are still zero. Triage in vCenter, not in SPL alone.

(7) ESA-only unfamiliar metrics in JSON after an upgrade — capture `| head` of one raw event, add field handling to a local macro for `dwpd` or other ESA-named checks, and retest the alert in dev.

(8) Stretched always shows a minor warning — witness and partition checks are path-sensitive; apply bump and NOC floor tuning from Step 4; do not set `severity=ok` in SPL with a hard mute.

(9) Duplicate `cluster` rows in results — you enabled both A and B; pick one as primary, or add `dedup` on 5-minute-rounded time and cluster for duplicate HEC events.

(10) vROps export uses `vsan_overall_health` not `vsan_health` — the `coalesce()` in the main SPL is the only wiring line you need; do not fork a second alert with different tier names.

(11) Resync in MB on one source and bytes on another — you already support `resync_unit`; if a legacy poller has no unit field, set `resync_unit=mb` in the HEC `event` once the SME shows a one-off JSON in dev.

(12) HEC 403 / wrong index — the HEC token is bound to an input that is not allowed to write `vxrail` — add the index to the HEC allowed list and redeploy; verify with a manual `curl` to HEC in dev.

## SPL

```spl
index=vxrail (sourcetype="vmware:vsan:health" OR sourcetype="vmware:vsan:powercli" OR sourcetype="vmware:vsan:health:log" OR sourcetype="vmware:vsan:vrops") earliest=-2h
| eval src_kind=case(sourcetype="vmware:vsan:health","rest", sourcetype="vmware:vsan:powercli","pcli", sourcetype="vmware:vsan:health:log","esxi_log", sourcetype="vmware:vsan:vrops","vrops", 1=1,"other")
| eval cluster=lower(coalesce(cluster_id, ClusterId, vsan_cluster_id, cluster_mo, cluster_moref, ClusterName, mo_id, "unknown"))
| eval health_raw=lower(trim(coalesce(vsan_health, OverallHealth, healthLevel, health_level, vsan_overall_health, vrops_vsan_status, "")))
| eval health_norm=case(match(health_raw,"^(healthy|green|ok)$"),"ok", match(health_raw,"^(info|blue|notice)$"),"info", match(health_raw,"^(warning|yellow|degraded|warn)$"),"warning", match(health_raw,"^(unhealthy|critical|red|error|failed|severe)$"),"critical", 1=1, health_raw)
| eval inacc=tonumber(coalesce(inaccessible_objects, InaccessibleObjects, ObjectsInaccessible, InaccessibleObjectCount, "0"))
| eval inacc_vm=tonumber(coalesce(inaccessible_vm_count, InaccessibleVmCount, inaccessibleVms, "0"))
| eval resync_bytes_raw=tonumber(coalesce(resync_bytes_remaining, ResyncBytesRemaining, BytesToSync, ResyncBytes, bytes_to_sync, "0"))
| eval resync_units=lower(coalesce(resync_unit, ResyncUnit, resync_uom, "bytes"))
| eval resync_bytes=if(resync_units="mb", resync_bytes_raw*1048576, if(resync_units="gb", resync_bytes_raw*1073741824, resync_bytes_raw))
| eval arch=lower(coalesce(vsan_architecture, VsanArchitecture, "osa"))
| eval is_stretched=tonumber(coalesce(is_stretched, IsStretched, witness_present, "0"))
| eval rebuild=tonumber(coalesce(rebuild_in_progress, RebuildInProgress, is_resync, "0"))
| where cluster!="unknown"
| stats values(src_kind) as sources, latest(health_norm) as health, max(inacc) as inacc, max(inacc_vm) as inacc_vm, max(resync_bytes) as resync_bytes, latest(arch) as arch, latest(is_stretched) as is_stretched, max(rebuild) as rebuild, latest(_time) as _time, earliest(eval(if(inacc>0, _time, null()))) as inacc_first, latest(eval(if(inacc>0, _time, null()))) as inacc_last by cluster
| eval inacc_age_min=if(inacc>0, round((inacc_last-inacc_first)/60,1), 0)
| lookup vsan_resync_thresholds.csv cluster OUTPUT critical_bytes, warning_bytes, watch_bytes, is_remote_consumer, stretched_inacc_bump
| eval is_remote=tonumber(coalesce(is_remote_consumer, "0"))
| eval inacc=if(is_remote=1 AND inacc>0, 0, inacc)
| eval stretched_bump=coalesce(tonumber(stretched_inacc_bump,10),0)
| eval critical_bytes=coalesce(tonumber(critical_bytes,10), 1099511627776)
| eval warning_bytes=coalesce(tonumber(warning_bytes,10), 107374182400)
| eval watch_bytes=coalesce(tonumber(watch_bytes,10), 10737418240)
| eval inacc_for_sev=if(is_stretched=1, inacc+stretched_bump, inacc)
| eval sev_inacc_crit=if(inacc_for_sev>0 AND inacc_age_min>=5,1,0)
| eval sev_inacc_warn=if(inacc_for_sev>0 AND inacc_age_min<5,1,0)
| eval severity_bucket=case(health="critical" OR sev_inacc_crit=1 OR resync_bytes>=critical_bytes OR inacc_vm>0, "critical", health="warning" OR sev_inacc_warn=1 OR (resync_bytes>=warning_bytes AND resync_bytes<critical_bytes), "warning", health="info" OR (resync_bytes>=watch_bytes AND resync_bytes<warning_bytes) OR rebuild=1, "watch", 1=1, "ok")
| where severity_bucket!="ok"
| eventstats count as fleet_cluster_rows, sum(eval(if(severity_bucket="critical",1,0))) as fleet_sev_critical, sum(eval(if(severity_bucket="warning",1,0))) as fleet_sev_warning, sum(eval(if(severity_bucket="watch",1,0))) as fleet_sev_watch
| eval resync_gb=round(resync_bytes/1073741824, 2)
| join type=left max=0 cluster [ search index=vxrail sourcetype="vxrail:disks" earliest=-4h | eval c=lower(coalesce(cluster_id,ClusterId,cluster_name,"unknown")) | eval pred=if(tonumber(coalesce(PredictiveFailureCount, predictive_failure, "0"))>0 OR match(lower(tostring(coalesce(predictive_failure,""))),"(?i)true|1|yes|pfa|predict"),1,0) | stats max(pred) as drive_predictive_active by c | rename c as cluster ]
| eval drive_vs_object_note=case(drive_predictive_active=1 AND resync_gb>0, "RMA/rebuild in progress (see UC-19.1.47)", drive_predictive_active=1, "Disk signals present; confirm object layer", resync_gb>0, "Resync without recent PFA; investigate", 1=1, "-")
| sort 0 - fleet_sev_critical - resync_gb
| table _time, fleet_cluster_rows, fleet_sev_critical, fleet_sev_warning, fleet_sev_watch, cluster, severity_bucket, health, inacc, inacc_age_min, inacc_vm, resync_gb, arch, is_stretched, drive_predictive_active, drive_vs_object_note, sources
```

## Visualization

Single value: `fleet_sev_critical`, `fleet_sev_warning`. Timechart: `resync_gb` by `cluster` (15m). Table: `cluster`, `severity_bucket`, `health`, `inacc`, `inacc_age_min`, `resync_gb`, `drive_predictive_active`, `drive_vs_object_note`. Drill: UC-19.1.47 disk view for the same `cluster`.

## Known False Positives

Planned vMotion or host evacuations: brief object churn. Planned SPBM changes: resync without failure. Planned drive RMA from UC-19.1.47: resync and reduced fault domain expected during rebuild. Node scale-out: rebalance and temporary unclaimed capacity. vSAN Skyline retest delay: warning lingers after fix until scan. vSAN data VLAN work: vmk or partition blips, often named in the UI. DRS or storage vMotion: temporary IO. ESA topologies: higher baseline resync in some designs — tune CSV. vSAN HCI Mesh: consumer sees remote producer maintenance. VxRail LCM rolling waves: per-wave resync. Proactive vSAN rebalance. Large snapshot delete: temporary component churn. Stretched design false positives: tune with bump, not off.
